text: Deployment Policy: All production deployments must go through the CI/CD pipeline. Deployments are only allowed between 9am and 5pm Pacific Time, Monday through Thursday. Friday deployments are prohibited unless approved by the VP of Engineering. Every deployment requires at least one peer review and must pass all automated tests.

Code Review Guidelines: Every pull request must be reviewed by at least one senior engineer. The review should check for code style, test coverage, security vulnerabilities, and performance implications. PRs with fewer than 50 lines can be approved by any team member. PRs with more than 400 lines must be broken into smaller chunks.

Team Meeting Notes (2026-06-15): Sprint planning for sprint 24. Agreed to focus on reducing technical debt in the auth module. Decision: Migrate from JWT to OAuth2 by end of Q3. Sarah will own the migration. Backend team will start implementation next sprint. Frontend changes will follow in sprint 26.

On-Call Rotation: The on-call schedule follows a weekly rotation. Each engineer covers one full week (Monday to Monday). Primary responsibilities include monitoring alerts, triaging incidents, and responding to pageouts within 15 minutes. The on-call engineer must not deploy to production during their on-call week.

Incident Response: Severity 1 incidents require immediate response. The on-call engineer has 5 minutes to acknowledge the page. If no response, the escalation chain starts: team lead after 5 minutes, manager after 10, director after 15. All S1 incidents must have a postmortem within 48 hours. The postmortem must identify root cause, action items, and preventions.

Logging Standards: All services must log to stdout in JSON format. Log levels: DEBUG for development, INFO for normal operations, WARNING for unexpected but handled issues, ERROR for failures requiring attention, CRITICAL for system-down events. Sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) must never be logged. Use structured logging with correlation IDs for request tracing.

Database Backup Policy: Production databases are backed up every 6 hours. Backups are retained for 30 days. Point-in-time recovery is enabled with transaction log backups every 5 minutes. Backup restoration drills are performed quarterly. The last drill (2026-05-20) confirmed RPO of 5 minutes and RTO of 2 hours.

API Design Guidelines: All new APIs must follow RESTful principles. Use plural nouns for resources (/users, /orders). Version APIs via URL prefix (/v1/, /v2/). Pagination uses cursor-based approach. Rate limiting is applied at 1000 requests per minute per client. All APIs must have OpenAPI 3.0 specifications before going to production.
